title: turnera caatingana
intro: Turnera caatingana, also known as the "Maracuja branca" or "white passionflower", is a plant species native to the Caatinga biome in northeastern Brazil. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's health benefits are thought to include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. In herbal preparations, Turnera caatingana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be found in supplements and capsules.
